mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present value of future earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, it signals severe labor market deterioration and imminent recessionary pressures, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.5%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue expansion and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during supply-constrained inflationary periods, or the mechanism predictive accuracy drops below 50% when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates sustained labor market strength and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple business cycles, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 225.0, it signals entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, triggering immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 60% of observations where CPIAUCSL surpasses 225.0, or the threshold effect disappears when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based aggregate demand recovery, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive power when GDPC1 growth decouples from RSAFS trends, or the interaction effect yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression testing.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment rates trigger sharp risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week, but this mechanism only activates during late-cycle expansions when labor market slack coincides with slowing economic momentum.
failure_condition: UNRATE levels fail to predict negative 5-day returns during late-cycle phases, or the signal produces positive returns in > 55% of triggered instances within the specified regime.
